consistency.notes: The spell description is consistent with standard 5th Edition D&D mechanics. The damage scaling, component costs, and action economy (action to cast, bonus action to repeat damage) are all standard for this spell.

quality.notes: The data quality is high; all required fields (slug, name, level, school, casting time, range, components, duration, description, classes) are present and correctly formatted.

mechanics.notes: Mechanically sound. It targets manufactured metal objects, deals initial fire damage on casting, and allows for repeated bonus action damage. The Constitution save on damage intake to potentially force a drop, and the resulting disadvantage penalty, are clear mechanical effects.
issues: The component description '(a piece of iron and a flame)' implies the flame is part of the material component, which might need clarification in a strict rules context (is the flame something the caster provides, or is it an environmental condition?). However, as raw data extraction, it is accurately recorded.
The description of the saving throw consequence (disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks) is clearly stated.

analysis.summary: The data for the 'Heat Metal' spell is consistent, high quality, and mechanically accurate according to established rules for this spell.
